introduction: This spec defines the persistent client configuration system for the `afc` CLI, enabling users to store hub URL and API key credentials in `$HOME/.af/config.toml` so that authenticated commands can be invoked without supplying flags or environment variables on every call. Note: requirements referencing `github.com/BurntSushi/toml` APIs are based on unverified PRD assumptions and should be cross-checked against the installed library version before implementation.

glossary.$HOME/.af/: The configuration directory for the afc CLI, created with permissions 0700.
glossary.$HOME/.af/config.toml: The canonical path to the persistent client configuration file for the afc CLI.
glossary.--api-key: CLI flag that sets the API key for a single invocation, taking highest precedence.
glossary.--hub-url: CLI flag that sets the hub URL for a single invocation, taking highest precedence.
glossary.AF_HUB_API_KEY: Environment variable that overrides the API key token from the config file.
glossary.AF_HUB_URL: Environment variable that overrides the hub URL from the config file.
glossary.POST /api/v1/auth/callback: The server-side auth callback endpoint that must be extended to return an api_key field alongside the user object.
glossary.[keys.<workspace_slug>]: A TOML table section that stores one API key entry (key_id, token, and optional label) for a given workspace slug.
glossary._login: The reserved workspace slug used to store the token obtained via `afc login`.
glossary.afc: The command-line interface tool being configured by this spec.
glossary.afc keys create: CLI command that creates a new API key for a workspace and stores it in the config file.
glossary.afc keys default: New CLI command introduced by this spec that sets the active workspace slug in the config file.
glossary.afc keys refresh: CLI command that rotates an existing API key token and updates it in the config file.
glossary.afc keys revoke: CLI command that removes an API key and its config file section.
glossary.afc login: CLI command that performs OAuth login and stores the returned token as `[keys._login]` in the config file.
glossary.api_key: Top-level TOML field storing the workspace slug that identifies the active API key section.
glossary.github.com/BurntSushi/toml: The TOML parsing and encoding library used by this project.
glossary.hub_url: Top-level TOML field storing the default hub URL used when no flag or environment variable overrides it.
glossary.key_id: The hex identifier for an API key, stored in a `[keys.<workspace_slug>]` section and used by `keys refresh` and `keys revoke` commands.
glossary.os.CreateTemp: Go standard library function used to create a temporary file for atomic config writes.
glossary.os.Rename: Go standard library function used to atomically replace the config file with the updated temp file.
glossary.token: The full plaintext API key string (e.g. `af_<key_id>_<secret>`) stored in a `[keys.<workspace_slug>]` TOML section.
glossary.workspace_slug: A short human-readable identifier for a workspace, used as the key in `[keys.<workspace_slug>]` TOML sections and matching the `--workspace` flag value.
glossary.label: An optional human-readable string in a `[keys.<workspace_slug>]` TOML section describing the key's purpose.
glossary.--workspace: CLI flag on `afc keys create` that specifies the workspace slug to scope the new API key to.
glossary.[keys._login]: The TOML section storing the token obtained via `afc login`, using the reserved workspace slug `_login`.
glossary.api_key.key: The `key` subfield within the `api_key` object returned by `POST /api/v1/auth/callback`, containing the full plaintext API key string.
glossary.key: In the context of the `api_key` response object, the full plaintext API key string (format `af_<key_id>_<secret>`).
glossary.<workspace-slug>: A positional argument representing a workspace slug value passed to `afc keys default`.
glossary.afc keys refresh <key-id>: CLI command that rotates the secret of an existing API key identified by its hex `key_id` and updates the matching config file entry.
glossary.afc keys revoke <key-id>: CLI command that permanently revokes an API key identified by its hex `key_id` and removes it from the config file.
glossary.afc keys default <workspace-slug>: CLI command that sets the `api_key` field in the config file to the specified workspace slug.
glossary.hub_url = \"\": The initial value of the `hub_url` field in a newly auto-created config file, representing an unset hub URL.
glossary.label = \"login\": The hardcoded label value assigned to the `[keys._login]` section when `afc login` stores credentials.

user_story.goal: have the config file and directory created automatically on first run
user_story.benefit: I do not need to manually create configuration infrastructure before using the CLI

acceptance_criteria.action: creates the `$HOME/.af/` directory with permissions 0700, then creates `$HOME/.af/config.toml` with permissions 0600 containing a comment header and `hub_url = ""`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: directory and file exist on disk with correct contents and permissions after startup
acceptance_criteria.trigger: starts up and `$HOME/.af/config.toml` does not exist

acceptance_criteria.action: makes no changes to the directory or file
sets permissions on `$HOME/.af/` to 0700 and on `$HOME/.af/config.toml` to 0600 only at creation time; it never modifies permissions on already-existing paths

edge_cases.action: exits with a non-zero status code and prints a descriptive error message identifying the failed directory creation
edge_cases.return_contract: non-zero exit code; error message printed to stderr
edge_cases.error_condition: `$HOME/.af/` directory cannot be created due to a permission denied or other OS error

edge_cases.action: exits with a non-zero status code and prints a descriptive error message identifying the failed file creation
edge_cases.return_contract: non-zero exit code; error message printed to stderr
edge_cases.error_condition: `$HOME/.af/config.toml` cannot be created (e.g. write permission denied on the directory)

edge_cases.action: removes the partial file before exiting with an error, ensuring no corrupt config file is left behind
edge_cases.return_contract: non-zero exit code; partial file cleaned up; error message printed to stderr
edge_cases.error_condition: auto-creation of `$HOME/.af/config.toml` fails partway (e.g. write interrupted), leaving a partial file on disk

requirements.title: Config file TOML structure and field definitions

user_story.goal: have a human-readable and hand-editable config file at a predictable location
user_story.benefit: I can inspect and modify my credentials without tooling

acceptance_criteria.action: stores `hub_url` as a top-level string field and `api_key` as a top-level string field referencing a `workspace_slug`, using TOML format parseable by `github.com/BurntSushi/toml`
stores each API key in a `[keys.<workspace_slug>]` TOML table section containing required string fields `key_id` and `token`, and optional string field `label`
treats an empty string value for `hub_url` or `api_key` in the config file as equivalent to the field being absent, falling through to the next resolution precedence level

edge_cases.action: exits immediately with a non-zero status code and prints a descriptive parse error message identifying the problem in the config file
edge_cases.return_contract: non-zero exit code; parse error message printed to stderr with file path and parse issue details
edge_cases.error_condition: `$HOME/.af/config.toml` exists but contains invalid TOML or unexpected field types

edge_cases.action: treats `api_key` as unset and falls through to the next resolution precedence level (flag, env var, or error)
edge_cases.error_condition: `api_key` references a `workspace_slug` for which no matching `[keys.<workspace_slug>]` section exists

requirements.title: Hub URL and API key resolution precedence

user_story.goal: have flags and environment variables always take precedence over the config file
user_story.benefit: I can override stored credentials for individual invocations without editing the config file

acceptance_criteria.action: resolves hub URL by checking, in order: `--hub-url` flag, `AF_HUB_URL` environment variable, `hub_url` config file field (empty string treated as unset), then exits with a descriptive error if none is found
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: resolved hub URL string, or non-zero exit code with error message referencing the config file, `--hub-url` flag, and `AF_HUB_URL` env var

acceptance_criteria.action: resolves the API key token by checking, in order: `--api-key` flag value used directly as token, `AF_HUB_API_KEY` environment variable used directly as token, then config file (reads `api_key` `workspace_slug` and retrieves `token` from matching `[keys.<workspace_slug>]` section), then exits with a descriptive error if none is found
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: resolved API key token string, or non-zero exit code with error message referencing the config file, `--api-key` flag, and `AF_HUB_API_KEY` env var

acceptance_criteria.action: prints an error message to stderr that mentions `$HOME/.af/config.toml` as an option alongside the relevant flags and environment variables
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: non-zero exit code; error message on stderr
acceptance_criteria.trigger: neither a flag, environment variable, nor populated config file entry provides the hub URL or API key

user_story.goal: have config file mutations be atomic
user_story.benefit: a crash or interruption during a write cannot leave the config file in a corrupt state

acceptance_criteria.action: writes all config file mutations by first encoding updated content to a temp file created via `os.CreateTemp` in `$HOME/.af/`, then atomically replacing `$HOME/.af/config.toml` via `os.Rename`
creates the temp file used for atomic writes with permissions 0600 before writing config content to it

edge_cases.action: removes the temp file via a deferred cleanup before returning an error; the original `$HOME/.af/config.toml` is left unchanged
edge_cases.return_contract: non-zero exit code; temp file removed; original config file intact; error message on stderr
edge_cases.error_condition: `os.Rename` fails after the temp file has been written during an atomic config update

edge_cases.action: returns an error without modifying the original `$HOME/.af/config.toml`
edge_cases.return_contract: non-zero exit code; original config file unchanged; error message on stderr
edge_cases.error_condition: `os.CreateTemp` fails (e.g. disk full or permission denied) during a config mutation

requirements.title: afc login stores credentials in config file

user_story.goal: have `afc login` automatically populate my config file with the returned token
user_story.benefit: I am ready for daily use immediately after login without manual file editing

acceptance_criteria.action: writes a `[keys._login]` section with `key_id`, `token` (from `api_key.key`), and `label = "login"` to `$HOME/.af/config.toml`, then sets `api_key` to `_login`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: config file updated; exit code 0; confirmation printed to stdout
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `afc login` completes successfully and the server responds via `POST /api/v1/auth/callback` with a user object and an `api_key` field containing `key` and `key_id`

acceptance_criteria.action: also writes the login hub URL (resolved from `--hub-url` flag or `AF_HUB_URL` env var) to `hub_url` in `$HOME/.af/config.toml`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: config file updated with `hub_url`; exit code 0
acceptance_criteria.condition: `hub_url` in the config file is currently empty or unset

acceptance_criteria.action: does not overwrite the existing `hub_url` value in `$HOME/.af/config.toml`
acceptance_criteria.condition: `hub_url` in the config file is already set to a non-empty value

edge_cases.action: exits with a non-zero status code and prints an error message indicating the login response was missing credential data; no config file changes are made
edge_cases.return_contract: non-zero exit code; error message on stderr; config file unchanged
edge_cases.error_condition: `POST /api/v1/auth/callback` response does not include an `api_key` field or the field is malformed

requirements.title: afc keys create updates config file

user_story.goal: have newly created API keys automatically saved to the config file
user_story.benefit: I can use a new key immediately without manually editing the config file

acceptance_criteria.action: adds a `[keys.<workspace_slug>]` section to `$HOME/.af/config.toml` containing `key_id`, `token`, and `label` fields, where `workspace_slug` comes from the `--workspace` flag
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: config file updated with new key section; exit code 0

requirements.title: afc keys refresh updates token in config file

user_story.goal: have a refreshed API key token automatically updated in the config file
user_story.benefit: I do not need to manually update the config file after rotating a key

acceptance_criteria.action: finds the `[keys.<workspace_slug>]` section in `$HOME/.af/config.toml` whose `key_id` matches the argument and updates its `token` field to the new value
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: config file updated with new token; exit code 0

edge_cases.action: logs a warning to stderr noting the key was not found in the config file, but does not exit with an error (the remote refresh may still have succeeded)
edge_cases.return_contract: exit code 0 if remote operation succeeded; warning on stderr about missing local config entry
edge_cases.error_condition: no `[keys.<workspace_slug>]` section in `$HOME/.af/config.toml` has a `key_id` matching the argument passed to `afc keys refresh`

requirements.title: afc keys revoke removes key section from config file

user_story.goal: have revoked API keys automatically removed from the config file
user_story.benefit: my config file stays clean and does not contain stale credentials

acceptance_criteria.action: finds and removes the `[keys.<workspace_slug>]` section from `$HOME/.af/config.toml` whose `key_id` matches the argument
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: config file updated with key section removed; exit code 0

acceptance_criteria.action: clears `api_key` to an empty string in `$HOME/.af/config.toml` and prints a warning to stderr instructing the user to set a new default with `afc keys default`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: config file updated; `api_key` set to empty string; warning printed to stderr; exit code 0
acceptance_criteria.condition: the revoked key's `workspace_slug` matches the current value of `api_key` in the config file

edge_cases.action: logs a warning to stderr noting the key was not found in the config file, but does not exit with an error
edge_cases.return_contract: exit code 0 if remote operation succeeded; warning on stderr about missing local config entry
edge_cases.error_condition: no `[keys.<workspace_slug>]` section in `$HOME/.af/config.toml` has a `key_id` matching the argument passed to `afc keys revoke`

requirements.title: afc keys default command sets active workspace slug

user_story.goal: be able to choose which stored API key is used by default
user_story.benefit: I can switch between workspaces without passing a flag on every invocation

acceptance_criteria.action: sets `api_key` to the specified `workspace_slug` in `$HOME/.af/config.toml` using an atomic write
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 0; config file updated; confirmation message on stdout
acceptance_criteria.trigger: user runs `afc keys default <workspace-slug>` and the specified `workspace_slug` has a matching `[keys.<workspace_slug>]` section in `$HOME/.af/config.toml`

acceptance_criteria.action: sets `api_key` to `_login` in `$HOME/.af/config.toml`, pointing to the login token section
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exit code 0; config file updated; confirmation message on stdout

edge_cases.action: exits with a non-zero status code and prints an error message; no changes are made to the config file
edge_cases.return_contract: non-zero exit code; error message on stderr; config file unchanged
edge_cases.error_condition: the `workspace_slug` argument to `afc keys default` does not match any existing `[keys.<workspace_slug>]` section in `$HOME/.af/config.toml`

edge_cases.action: prints usage help and exits with a non-zero status code
edge_cases.return_contract: non-zero exit code; usage message on stderr
edge_cases.error_condition: no `<workspace-slug>` argument is provided to `afc keys default`

requirements.title: Server-side auth callback returns API key

user_story.goal: receive an API key token from the server after logging in
user_story.benefit: the CLI can automatically store my credentials without a separate step

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a JSON response containing both the user object and an `api_key` object with `key` (full token string) and `key_id` (hex identifier) fields
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a successful OAuth callback is processed at `POST /api/v1/auth/callback`

requirements.title: Backward compatibility with existing flags and environment variables

user_story.goal: continue using `--hub-url`, `--api-key`, `AF_HUB_URL`, and `AF_HUB_API_KEY` exactly as before
user_story.benefit: I experience no behavior change from the introduction of config file support

acceptance_criteria.action: continues to accept `--hub-url`, `--api-key`, `AF_HUB_URL`, and `AF_HUB_API_KEY` with the same semantics as before this feature, with flags and env vars always taking precedence over config file values
uses the flag or env var value for the hub URL without reading `hub_url` from `$HOME/.af/config.toml`
acceptance_criteria.state: a valid `--hub-url` flag or `AF_HUB_URL` env var is present

acceptance_criteria.action: uses the flag or env var value directly as the API key token without reading from `$HOME/.af/config.toml`
acceptance_criteria.state: a valid `--api-key` flag or `AF_HUB_API_KEY` env var is present

correctness_properties.title: Flag and env var always override config file
correctness_properties.for_any: afc CLI invocation where `--hub-url` or `AF_HUB_URL` is set to a non-empty value
correctness_properties.invariant: the resolved hub URL equals the flag or env var value, never the `hub_url` field from `$HOME/.af/config.toml`

correctness_properties.title: Config file permissions are always restrictive at creation
correctness_properties.invariant: the directory has permissions 0700 and the file has permissions 0600 immediately after creation

correctness_properties.title: Atomic writes never leave a partial config file
correctness_properties.for_any: any config mutation operation (login, keys create, keys refresh, keys revoke, keys default)
correctness_properties.invariant: at no point in time does `$HOME/.af/config.toml` contain partially written content; the file transitions atomically from old to new content via `os.Rename`

correctness_properties.title: Empty string config values fall through to next precedence level
correctness_properties.for_any: config file where `hub_url` or `api_key` is set to an empty string
correctness_properties.invariant: the empty string is treated identically to the field being absent; resolution continues to the next level and does not short-circuit with an empty value

correctness_properties.title: Only designated commands modify the config file
correctness_properties.for_any: any `afc` command that is not `afc login`, `afc keys create`, `afc keys refresh`, `afc keys revoke`, or `afc keys default`
correctness_properties.invariant: `$HOME/.af/config.toml` is not modified (no writes, no permission changes, no structural changes) during the command's execution

correctness_properties.title: keys default rejects non-existent workspace slugs
correctness_properties.for_any: `workspace_slug` argument to `afc keys default` that has no matching `[keys.<workspace_slug>]` section in `$HOME/.af/config.toml`
correctness_properties.invariant: `api_key` in the config file is not modified and the command exits with a non-zero status code

execution_paths.title: First-time user: login then run authenticated command

steps.action: runs `afc login --hub-url https://hub.example.com`
detects `$HOME/.af/config.toml` does not exist, creates `$HOME/.af/` (0700) and `$HOME/.af/config.toml` (0600) with `hub_url = ""`
initiates OAuth flow and receives callback from server at `POST /api/v1/auth/callback`
returns JSON response with user object and `api_key` containing `key` and `key_id` fields

writes `[keys._login]` section with `key_id`, `token`, and `label = "login"` to `$HOME/.af/config.toml` via atomic write; sets `api_key = "_login"`; writes `hub_url = "https://hub.example.com"` since it was empty
prints confirmation to stdout; exits with code 0
runs `afc workspace list` (no flags)
reads `$HOME/.af/config.toml`, resolves hub URL from `hub_url` field and API key token from `[keys._login].token`
executes the workspace list command using resolved credentials; returns results to user

execution_paths.title: User creates a workspace key and sets it as default

steps.action: runs `afc keys create --workspace my-project`
resolves credentials from `$HOME/.af/config.toml` (hub URL and active key token) and calls the remote API to create a new key
on success, writes `[keys.my-project]` section with `key_id`, `token`, and `label` to `$HOME/.af/config.toml` via atomic write
runs `afc keys default my-project`
verifies `[keys.my-project]` exists in `$HOME/.af/config.toml`, then sets `api_key = "my-project"` via atomic write
prints confirmation to stdout; exits with code 0
runs `afc keys revoke a1b2c3d4e5f6`
resolves credentials from `$HOME/.af/config.toml` and calls the remote API to revoke the key
finds `[keys.my-project]` section with matching `key_id = "a1b2c3d4e5f6"` in `$HOME/.af/config.toml`
removes `[keys.my-project]` section; detects that `api_key` was `"my-project"` (the revoked slug); clears `api_key` to empty string; writes updated config atomically
prints warning to stderr instructing user to run `afc keys default` to set a new active key; exits with code 0

execution_paths.title: User with existing flags continues working unchanged

steps.action: runs `afc workspace list --hub-url https://hub.example.com --api-key af_abc123_secret`
resolves hub URL from `--hub-url` flag (highest precedence) and API key from `--api-key` flag (highest precedence), bypassing config file for these values
executes the workspace list command using flag-provided credentials; returns results to user

execution_paths.title: Malformed config file causes immediate exit

steps.action: runs any `afc` command when `$HOME/.af/config.toml` contains invalid TOML
attempts to parse `$HOME/.af/config.toml` using `github.com/BurntSushi/toml`
detects parse error; prints descriptive error message to stderr identifying the file path and parse issue; exits with non-zero status code

error_handling.condition: `$HOME/.af/` directory cannot be created due to OS permission or I/O error
error_handling.behavior: afc CLI exits with non-zero status code and prints a descriptive error message to stderr identifying the failed directory creation and the path

error_handling.condition: `$HOME/.af/config.toml` cannot be created during auto-creation (e.g. write permission denied)
error_handling.behavior: afc CLI exits with non-zero status code and prints a descriptive error message to stderr

error_handling.condition: Auto-creation of `$HOME/.af/config.toml` fails midway leaving a partial file
error_handling.behavior: afc CLI removes the partial file before exiting with a non-zero status code and error message on stderr

error_handling.condition: `$HOME/.af/config.toml` contains invalid TOML or unexpected field types
error_handling.behavior: afc CLI exits immediately with non-zero status code and prints a descriptive parse error message to stderr identifying the file path and nature of the parse failure
afc CLI treats `api_key` as unset and falls through to the next resolution level; if no level provides a value, exits with a descriptive error referencing the config file

error_handling.condition: Hub URL not found via flag, `AF_HUB_URL`, or config file
error_handling.behavior: afc CLI exits with non-zero status code and prints an error to stderr mentioning `$HOME/.af/config.toml`, the `--hub-url` flag, and `AF_HUB_URL` env var as options

error_handling.condition: API key token not found via flag, `AF_HUB_API_KEY`, or config file
error_handling.behavior: afc CLI exits with non-zero status code and prints an error to stderr mentioning `$HOME/.af/config.toml`, the `--api-key` flag, and `AF_HUB_API_KEY` env var as options

error_handling.condition: `os.Rename` fails after writing temp file during atomic config update
error_handling.behavior: afc CLI removes the temp file via deferred cleanup, leaves original `$HOME/.af/config.toml` unchanged, exits with non-zero status and error on stderr
afc CLI returns an error without modifying the original config file; exits with non-zero status and error message on stderr

error_handling.condition: `POST /api/v1/auth/callback` response is missing or has a malformed `api_key` field after `afc login`
error_handling.behavior: afc CLI exits with non-zero status code and prints an error to stderr indicating the login response lacked credential data; config file is not modified

error_handling.condition: `afc keys revoke` is called for a `key_id` not found in any `[keys.<workspace_slug>]` section of the config file
error_handling.behavior: afc CLI logs a warning to stderr that the key was not found locally but does not exit with an error if the remote operation succeeded

error_handling.condition: `afc keys default` is called with a `workspace_slug` that has no matching `[keys.<workspace_slug>]` section
error_handling.behavior: afc CLI exits with non-zero status code and prints an error to stderr; the config file is not modified

error_handling.condition: `afc keys default` is called with no `<workspace-slug>` argument
error_handling.behavior: afc CLI prints usage help to stderr and exits with a non-zero status code

error_handling.condition: `afc keys revoke` removes the key whose `workspace_slug` matches the current `api_key` default
error_handling.behavior: afc CLI clears `api_key` to empty string in the config file and prints a warning to stderr instructing the user to run `afc keys default` to set a new default
